title: RFC 9000 Address-Validation Token Emission Work Item
summary: Keep the RFC 9000 path-validation slice honest while the runtime gains server-only NEW_TOKEN emission and per-address deduplication.
planned_changes:
- Add a server-only token-emission record keyed by remote address.
- Flush NEW_TOKEN frames after new-address validation once a protected 1-RTT send is possible.
- Keep repeated validation of the same address from creating duplicate NEW_TOKEN traffic.
- Keep the framing helper internal to the runtime and avoid public API widening.
verification_plan: Run the RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for the two token-emission clauses and confirm the new-token payload helper round-trips through the frame codec.
completion_notes: Completed for the server-only NEW_TOKEN emission slice. Requirement-home tests prove one token per validated address, keep client-role validation from emitting NEW_TOKEN traffic, and preserve the existing frame codec round-trip helper.
out_of_scope: Retry-token handling or any broader token-management API.
Public API widening for token issuance or token validation.
